title: CandidateFalsifiers
description: Set 069 S5 - the quality-gated ceiling->floor promotion ratchet artifact. Written as candidate-falsifiers.json beside spec.md at the session-set root by a set whose automated pull critic reproduced one or more probeable defects. Each candidate is a falsifier proposed for promotion into the deterministic floor (the Set 068 contract gate) - NEVER auto-merged. The runtime gate is the pure-Python validator ai_router.floor_ratchet.validate_candidate_falsifiers_artifact, which additionally computes per-candidate admission via ai_router.floor_ratchet.admission_decision (the six quality gates: fails-on-old, passes-on-fixed, drives-a-public-contract, survives an N-run flake check, has an owner, carries human sign-off). A candidate is admitted to the floor ONLY when every mechanical gate passes AND humanSignoff.status == 'approved'; 'waived' is an explicit operator decision not to promote; 'pending' is the only state the builder emits (never auto-merge). See docs/proposals/2026-06-16-pull-architecture-capabilities/proposal.md Sec.1.5.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 = the v1 contract locked in Set 069 S5.

sessionSetName.description: The slug of the session set this artifact covers (the session-set directory basename). The close-out coverage check confirms it matches the set being closed.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes about the candidate library or promotion decisions.

candidates.description: The candidate falsifiers proposed for floor promotion. At least one.

CandidateFalsifier.description: One falsifier proposed for promotion to the floor, built from a reproduced probeable defect. id is set-unique. defect names the reproduced finding. falsifier is the TRUSTED probe (commandId XOR templateId + typed args) drawn from the reproduced transcript - never model-authored argv. entrypoint + contractKind enforce that the falsifier drives a public contract. failsOnOld/passesOnFixed are the recorded differential; flakeCheck is the N-run majority; owner + humanSignoff are the human gate.

id.description: A stable, set-unique id for this candidate (e.g. 'CF1').

findingRef.description: A stable reference to the reproduced finding this falsifier covers (e.g. '<provider>:<index>' from the critique artifact, or the finding's description).

severity.description: Optional severity, preserved from the finding.

falsifier.description: The trusted probe that becomes the floor falsifier. Exactly one of commandId / templateId (the trusted-probe XOR), plus optional typed args. Never model-authored argv.

entrypoint.description: The real PUBLIC entrypoint the falsifier drives (meta-oracle rule). The admission gate requires kind to be one of the public kinds (agent_harness is not admissible).

contractKind.description: Whether the falsifier asserts on a public contract or an incidental string/timing. Only 'public_contract' is admissible.

failsOnOld.description: The recorded run of the falsifier on the pre-fix ref. Admission requires failed=true on a named ref.

passesOnFixed.description: The recorded run of the falsifier on the fixed ref (must differ from failsOnOld.ref). Admission requires passed=true on a named ref.

flakeCheck.description: The N-run flake check. Admission requires runs >= the configured minimum (default 3), stable=true, and agreeing a majority of runs.

owner.description: The human who owns this falsifier.

humanSignoff.description: The human gate. status 'pending' (the only state the builder emits) -> not promoted; 'approved' (a human writes it; requires 'by') + all gates pass -> admitted; 'waived' -> an explicit decision not to promote (requires 'note'). A candidate is NEVER auto-merged.
